esxiMigration.migrationTypeColdDesc: VM must be powered off. Most reliable method, ideal for batch migrations.
esxiMigration.migrationTypeLiveDesc: VM stays running during disk transfer. Requires SSH access to ESXi host.
esxiMigration.migrationTypeSshfsBootDesc: Near-zero downtime: VM boots on Proxmox from remote ESXi disks within seconds. Experimental: the guest must boot unattended on the target controller; may fail for EFI Windows or guests needing driver injection. Prefer Live or Offline for production.
esxiMigration.migrationTypeSshfsBootExperimentalWarnin: SSHFS Boot is experimental and only works when the guest boots correctly on the target without driver adaptation. For EFI Windows, vCenter-sourced VMs, or anything production-critical, choose Live or Offline.
esxiMigration.sshfsBootMigrationInfo: VM is stopped on VMware and immediately booted on Proxmox from the ESXi datastore via SSHFS/SSH. Disks are live-copied to local storage using drive-mirror while the VM runs. Total downtime: seconds.
esxiMigration.liveMigrationInfo: Disks are cloned on ESXi via vmkfstools while the VM runs, then transferred to Proxmox. The VM is powered off automatically for final cutover (minimal downtime).
esxiMigration.sshRequired: SSH is not configured on the target Proxmox connection. SSH access is required to transfer disks and configure the VM. Please enable SSH in the connection settings.
esxiMigration.sshfsNotInstalled: sshfs is not installed on the target Proxmox node. SSHFS and SSHFS Boot modes require it. Install with: apt install sshfs
esxiMigration.installFailedTitle: Install failed on the target node
esxiMigration.installFailed401EnterpriseHint: Your APT is configured against the enterprise Proxmox repos but no valid subscription is attached (HTTP 401). Either fix your subscription credentials in /etc/apt/auth.conf.d/, or switch the affected nodes to the no-subscription repo and click Install again:
esxiMigration.vsanRequiresVcenter: Source VM has disks on vSAN ({datastores}). vSAN datastores are not supported through a direct ESXi connection because vSAN objects require the NFC protocol, which is only available via vCenter. Please add a vCenter connection that manages this ESXi host and run the migration from there.
esxiMigration.windowsVirtioNote: Windows guest: the VM will boot on Proxmox with SATA disk and e1000 NIC (built-in Windows drivers). For better disk and network performance, install the VirtIO drivers inside the VM after it boots. Download: https://fedorapeople.org/groups/virt/virtio-win/direct-downloads/stable-virtio/virtio-win.iso — install guide: https://pve.proxmox.com/wiki/Windows_VirtIO_Drivers

esxiMigration.enterpriseRequiredDesc: VMware to Proxmox migration is available on the Enterprise plan. Upgrade your license to unlock this feature.

recs.heading: Security recommendations
recs.intro: For lab or single-admin setups, root is acceptable if SSH keys are properly managed. For production, multi-admin, or compliance-sensitive environments, a dedicated user with sudo is recommended: you get an audit trail in /var/log/auth.log and compromise is scoped to the whitelisted commands.
recs.step1Title: Create a dedicated SSH user on each PVE node
recs.step1Hint: Run these commands on every PVE node (adapt the public key to yours).
recs.step2Hint: Copy the template, paste it into /etc/sudoers.d/proxcenter on every PVE node, and validate with visudo -c.
recs.step3Title: Switch the ProxCenter connection to the new user
recs.step3Hint: In Settings > Connections > PVE, edit each connection, change the SSH user to proxcenter, and enable Use sudo.
recs.disclaimer: Note: the command allowlist is applied when commands route through the embedded orchestrator. Some code paths (migrations, direct node operations) bypass this check today. A security-hardening task is tracked to extend strict enforcement; running ProxCenter under a dedicated user with sudo already provides OS-level audit trail (/var/log/auth.log) and scopes compromise impact.
recs.shellWrapWarning: A few commands use shell constructs (for-loops over OVS bridges) that cannot be expressed as simple sudoers exec rules. The template includes a scoped /bin/sh entry for these. If you prefer to avoid /bin/sh in sudoers, do not use the Network Flows sFlow configuration feature.

drsPage.helpMemoryHigh: Nodes above this memory usage are considered overloaded. DRS will try to move VMs away from them.
drsPage.helpMemoryLow: Nodes below this memory usage are considered underloaded and can receive VMs from overloaded nodes.
drsPage.helpImbalanceThreshold: Standard deviation of resource usage across nodes. When exceeded, DRS proactively balances even if no node is above the high threshold. Lower value = more aggressive balancing.
drsPage.helpHomogenization: When enabled, DRS continuously works to equalize load across all nodes, not just when thresholds are exceeded. This is the recommended mode for most clusters.
drsPage.helpMaxLoadSpread: Maximum acceptable gap (in %) between the most and least loaded nodes. If the spread exceeds this value, DRS generates migration recommendations to reduce it.
drsPage.helpEnableAffinity: When enabled, DRS considers affinity rules (from Proxmox tags or manual rules) when generating recommendations. VMs with affinity/anti-affinity constraints will be placed accordingly.
drsPage.helpEnforceAffinity: When enforced, DRS will never recommend a migration that violates an affinity rule, even if it would improve balance. When disabled, rules are preferred but not mandatory.
drsPage.helpMaxConcurrent: Maximum number of VM migrations that can run simultaneously. Higher values balance faster but put more load on the network.
drsPage.helpMaxConcurrentPerCluster: Caps how many migrations can be active in a single cluster at once. Set to 0 to disable. Prevents one busy cluster from monopolizing the global slot pool when several clusters need rebalancing.
drsPage.helpMaxTargetInflow: Caps how many migrations may target the same node in one rebalance cycle. Set to 0 to disable. Prevents flooding the least-loaded node with several simultaneous migrations.
drsPage.helpCooldown: Minimum wait time between two migrations of the same VM. Prevents a VM from being bounced back and forth.
drsPage.helpMaxPending: Maximum number of pending recommendations. Oldest recommendations are auto-dismissed when this limit is exceeded.
drsPage.concurrentMigrationWarningDesc: In automatic or partial mode, recommendations are calculated on the current cluster state. When multiple migrations run simultaneously, later migrations may be based on an outdated state. The orchestrator will re-evaluate after each batch completes to correct any suboptimal placements.
drsPage.helpLargerFirst: When enabled, DRS prioritizes migrating VMs with the highest resource usage first. This can resolve imbalances faster but migrations take longer.
drsPage.helpPreventOverprov: When enabled, DRS will not migrate a VM to a node if it would cause that node to exceed its capacity (allocated > available). Prevents overcommitting resources.
drsPage.helpResourceWeights: How much each resource type influences the balancing score.
drsPage.helpResourceWeightsDesc: Set to 0 to ignore a resource. Set to 2 for maximum priority. For example, Memory=1 CPU=0.3 Storage=0 means DRS focuses primarily on memory balance.
